fix(import): handle phone uniqueness conflicts + speed up preview progress (#1093)

* fix(import): handle phone uniqueness conflicts during order commit + speed up preview progress

Two fixes for the import job pipeline:

1. Customer phone dedup (resolve-import-customer.ts): Fix customers_merchant_phone_unique
   constraint violation during order commit. Phone-only orders now link to existing phone
   holders with email/user_id. Email-identified orders INSERT with phone=null when phone
   is already taken. Safety net retries INSERT without phone on concurrent 23505 errors.

2. Preview progress cold-start (import-job-service.ts): Report totalRows to the UI as
   soon as CSV is parsed, before existing orders/products DB queries finish. This closes
   the gap where the UI shows 0/0 while waiting for unrelated DB loads.

Summary:
- add user directory domain, repo, service, and HTTP API flows for
  listing users, inspecting identities, and auditable status
  transitions

  idempotent updates, and fail-closed email collision handling

  directory with explicit lifecycle controls and auditability

  avoiding unsupported auto-link and merge behavior

  webhook or SCIM follow-up work has a clear contract

commit.message:
Strip the presentation back to a simpler publication baseline

The interaction web idea is better than the previous decorative passes,
but the surrounding shell still carried too much visual baggage. This
reset removes the leftover kinetic-home scripts and rewrites the global,
note, tag, and graph styles around a quieter baseline so the content and
note-to-note interaction become the primary visual focus.

The result is a lighter, flatter publication system: simple surfaces,
clean borders, calmer navigation, and less ornamental contrast. The home
interaction web remains, while the rest of the site now shares a much
more restrained reading-first style.

Constraint: The homepage note-web behavior had to keep working while the rest of the styling was simplified
Constraint: Existing routes and server-side templates remained the source of truth
Rejected: Keep the dark glossy shell and only tweak colors | still felt over-designed
Rejected: Remove the note web as well | it was the one interaction direction the user explicitly wanted to keep
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: moderate
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Add visual complexity only when it directly improves note discovery or reading; avoid standalone flourishes

commit.message:
Turn the blog into a kinetic editorial surface

The previous pass improved structure but still looked like a themed app.
This rework pushes the blog into a much more opinionated publication
shape: dark shell, paper reading surfaces, a stronger hierarchy, and a
homepage cover that behaves like an animated editorial spread instead of
just another content dashboard.

Pretext now does visibly heavier lifting on the landing page. The hero
headline is rendered as measured lines inside a custom stage with moving
orb accents, while the masonry stream keeps using measured copy for
stable card packing. I also added a small reveal layer so archive and
news cards settle into place instead of appearing flatly.

Constraint: Content, routes, and markdown rendering had to stay intact
Constraint: The redesign had to reuse the existing static Rust pipeline
Rejected: Keep iterating on the earlier pastel card design | it still read like a template rather than a publication
Rejected: Animate the entire markdown article body with Pretext | too risky for readability and accessibility
Confidence: medium
Scope-risk: broad
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Keep Pretext concentrated on headline, card, and accent surfaces; if you expand the animated layout further, preserve no-JS fallbacks and readability first

commit.message:
Work (#9)

* Add contacts import (Google Sheets/YClients), DB migrations, and clients/staff/settings UI/hooks updates

### Motivation
- Provide a robust path to import contacts from either YClients or Google Sheets and persist import metadata in org settings.
- Make clients UI more feature-rich (age/discount filters, wider table with left-accent scrollbar) and keep staff UI in sync with real masters data from the DB.
- Ensure database schema supports YClients-compatible client fields and automatic linking between user records and clients when YClients IDs appear.

### Description
- Added an API endpoint `app/api/clients/import/route.ts` that normalizes rows from `yclients` or `google_sheets`, upserts/inserts into `clients`, and writes import metadata into `org_settings`.
- Implemented `lib/imports/normalizeGoogleSheetsClient.ts` to adapt Google Sheets rows into the clients schema and added `normalizeYclientsRow` logic in the import route.
- Introduced DB migrations and helper SQL: `add_contacts_import_source.sql`, `add_yclients_clients_columns.sql`, `link_users_to_clients_on_yc_id.sql`, seed and reset updates and a seeds file `seed_test_masters.sql` to populate masters for staff cards.
- Updated frontend: extended clients filters and table (`app/clients/page.tsx`) with age/discount filters, redesigned table layout and local left-accent scrollbar (`app/globals.css`), added settings UI to pick and save import source (`app/settings/page.tsx`), and disabled/marked in-development auto-systems in `app/system/page.tsx`.
- Refactored data hooks: `lib/hooks/useClients.ts` now reads from `clients` and normalizes new fields (discount, channels, birthday, etc.), and added a new `lib/hooks/useYclientsMasters.ts` hook to fetch/subscribe to `masters` for the staff UI; `app/staff/page.tsx` updated to consume masters and render realistic KPIs and cards.

### Testing
- Ran TypeScript/Next.js build (`next build`) and static type-checking locally and the build completed without type errors.
- Ran linter/format checks (ESLint) and no lint errors were reported for modified files.
- Performed basic automated smoke checks by running the dev server and loading `Clients`, `Staff`, and `Settings` pages to ensure hooks and new components render without runtime errors (no automated unit tests were changed or present for these new features).

* Store messenger user IDs on clients during yc_id auto-link

* Add reverse sync from clients to messenger user tables

commit.message:
fix: stabilize Project AI conversation ownership across origins (#571)

* fix(chat): stabilize project conversation ownership

Summary:
- route persistent project conversation APIs through a stable owner
  resolver that uses the authenticated human in OIDC mode and the
  server-defined `local-user:default` principal when auth is disabled
- normalize legacy non-OIDC conversation owners during list/get/watch so
  existing project conversations remain visible after switching frontend
  ports or losing browser-local state
- stop sending `X-OpenASE-Chat-User` on persistent project conversation
  requests, document the auth-disabled fallback, and refresh the
  controller restore tests around mux streaming

Rationale:
- project conversation ownership must come from the server instead of a
  browser-local random id, otherwise port changes create a new owner
  namespace and break restore/list semantics
- auth-disabled mode is a local-instance fallback, so a stable shared
  principal is safer and more explainable than coupling persistence to
  `localStorage`

Tests:
- PATH=$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$PATH go test ./internal/httpapi ./internal/chat ./internal/repo/chatconversation
- PATH=$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/lib/api/chat.test.ts src/lib/features/chat/project-conversation-controller-restore.test.ts src/lib/features/chat/project-conversation-controller-restore-live.test.ts
